items.title: Welcome
items.body:
a small place for things i find beautiful, interesting, or worth sharing.

no real theme. just a collection of moments that made me stop and look a little longer.

items.title: Stealing non-Perfectionism
items.caption: Starting simple. I just copied/stole the quote design, and added the side belt of design - simple start

items.title: Textured textile tech walls
items.caption: This was first time, I played with textures digitally. I was drawn to textures that are more familiar, but absent from our representations in digital world.

items.title: Selective seeing
items.caption: Stealing, cutting and zooming into images to show new ways of seeing. Historian-esque, ofcourse.

items.title: Textures - A thread
items.caption: While finding my style in Digital collage-ing. I felt a lack. Something was missing... when I found good textures, I knew that I missed textures in all my scrolling Insta and YouTube.

items.title: Simple flat patterns
items.caption: Flat patterns is how I started as well. The things that are abound in all the corporate-ised posts in all my social media feeds. The gradients - a harsh reminder.

items.title: Where is this frustration coming from?
items.body:
The digital design world I saw for last decades has been flat. FLAT! The whole of my instagram and internet is flat - just minimalist and flat. 

But, the world outside, in India, is so rich in texture. I missed that texture - textures contain stories, emotions and memory.

items.title: Picking Textile Dreams w/ Mamma
items.caption: Colors, textiles, and asethetics with Mamma - clearly we both have quite different taste at times, but we share so much. So immemorialising a few shared ones.

items.title: The Patterned Skies
items.caption: In a world if I imagine, the skies would have changing textile patterns

items.title: Patterned Skies 2
items.caption: In a world if I imagine, the skies would have changing textile patterns

items.title: Thank you!
items.body:
Let me know what you think, or if you want to know more how I created them. 

I used Affinity in my mac to make these. More than anything, they were generally quite fun to make.
